summary: The implementation covers most requirements with frontend pages, backend data models, and core functionality for product catalog, shopping cart, orders, and inventory. However, several features are missing from the diff evidence, including the required generated product images and some acceptance criteria details.

missing_requirements.requirement: Render generated product images as static assets in the frontend public directory
missing_requirements.reason: No evidence of generated images being placed in frontend/public/assets/generated. The diff shows ProductImage component and imageHelpers utility, but no actual image files (hero-banner.dim_1200x400.png, product-phone.dim_400x400.png, product-laptop.dim_400x400.png, product-headphones.dim_400x400.png, product-watch.dim_400x400.png, cart-icon.dim_128x128.png, order-success.dim_128x128.png) in the public directory.

missing_requirements.requirement: Order status can be tracked (pending, confirmed, shipped, delivered)
missing_requirements.reason: Backend OrderStatus type defines pending, shipped, delivered, and cancelled, but the BuildRequest specifies 'confirmed' as a status which is not present in the implementation. The diff shows #pending, #shipped, #delivered, #cancelled but no #confirmed status.

While InventoryDashboardPage is referenced in App.tsx routing, the actual page implementation is not visible in the diff (file was omitted due to limits). Cannot verify if dashboard shows current inventory levels as required.

StockIndicator component shows 'Low Stock' badge for stock <= threshold, but no evidence of this being used in an inventory dashboard context. The catalog uses it, but the requirement specifically mentions dashboard visual indicators.

hallucinated_features.description: User profile management system with name, email, and phone fields
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend includes UserProfile type and profile management methods (getCallerUserProfile, getUserProfile, saveCallerUserProfile) which were not requested in any requirement. ProfileSetupModal forces users to complete profile before using the app.
Backend includes addProduct, updateProduct, and deleteProduct methods with admin-only authorization. BuildRequest explicitly lists 'Admin dashboard for product management' as a non-goal.

hallucinated_features.description: Theme provider with dark mode support
hallucinated_features.reason: App.tsx includes ThemeProvider with dark/light mode switching capability via next-themes. No requirement specified theme switching or dark mode support.
Footer component includes links to X (Twitter), Facebook, and Instagram. No requirement requested social media integration or links.
